Lycopodium is a genus of small, club-mosses that are primitive vascular plants. The Lycopodium genus has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its purported health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antitumor properties, which may help alleviate respiratory issues, skin conditions, and certain types of cancer. The bioactive constituents of Lycopodium include alkaloids, saponins, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
